action: The `evolve release` pipeline's rebuild-binary step re-pins state.json:expected_ship_sha to the freshly built binary's hash but the rebuilt go/evolve never lands in the release commit — v18.3.0 (journal 18.3.0-20260610T104142Z.json) reported rebuild-binary=ok and pinned d49caae8…, yet release commit 561f78ff contained only the 6 version-bump/changelog files and the disk binary stayed at the 18.2.0 build (19cb9d19…), guaranteeing SELF_SHA_TAMPERED on the next ship. Operator had to run the runtime-reference deterministic double-build dance + del(.expected_ship_sha) + ship --class manual (233d302e) post-release. Fix direction: either (a) rebuild-binary stages go/evolve so the release ship commits it (then the pin matches HEAD:go/evolve), or (b) the step must NOT re-pin a sha for a binary it does not commit — pin only after the binary is at HEAD. Acceptance: after `evolve release X.Y.Z`, shasum of tracked go/evolve == expected_ship_sha == sha of HEAD:go/evolve blob, and `./go/evolve --version` reports X.Y.Z at the release commit.
evidence_pointer: .evolve/release-journal/18.3.0-20260610T104142Z.json (rebuild-binary ok); git show 561f78ff --stat (no go/evolve); post-release pin d49caae8… vs disk 19cb9d19…; remediation commit 233d302e
